title: No multi-conversation / session isolation — all context bleeds into one stream
what_failed: Pi has no concept of separate conversation threads. Everything is one flat context. User wants ChatGPT/Claude-style isolated chats with their own memory bubbles.
suggested_fix: Implement conversation_id scoping. Each session gets a unique ID. L3 context loads globally but per-session short-term memory is isolated. Add 'new chat' command that resets session context without wiping L3.
migration_plan: 2. L3 category new_chat clears session_history entries on /newchat command.
3. _get_system_prompt_split warm_p scopes session_history by current conversation_id.

4. new-chat command: reset self.messages/self.history, generate new conversation_id, clear session_history in SQLite (set invalid_at=now for session_history rows), keep all other L3 categories intact.
6. This is a 1-week effort — do not implement mid-sprint.
implementation_notes: Architectural — minimum 1 week. Do not implement in isolation. Dependency: L3 must support conversation_id column (PRAGMA add). Requires /newchat command in pi_agent.py run loop.
progress: 2026-05-29: /newchat increment SHIPPED - conversation_id field + new-chat command resets self.messages/history without wiping L3 (tests in test_conversation_golden.py). REMAINING: per-conversation L3 scoping + Telegram /newchat wiring. | 2026-05-30: FOUNDATION shipped - l3_cache.conversation_id column added; memory_write records conversation_id (composes with the /newchat conversation_id from prior increment). REMAINING: scope L3 reads by conversation_id + pi_agent stamps self.conversation_id on writes + Telegram /newchat.
